meaning: n.失败,不及格vi.失败,放弃,考试不及格vt.使不及格
examples: If he doesn't find a solution to his problem soon, he'll surely flunk out of college.
If he is no good, flunk him.
LL: I said you are going to flunk the students.
Any business school would flunk you in a minute for spending money on custom screws.
The headline of a recent article on Harvard Business Review: Marketers Flunk the Big Data Test.
